uplink.description: measurement frame: motion detected (digital input 0 high)
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds frame; byte0=0xA0 selects a non-timestamped measurement frame, block 0x01 carries the digital-input states (bit0=input0=PIR detector, high=motion). Expected produced by running reference/upstream-codec.js (entree.value[0][0]=1).

uplink.description: measurement frame: no motion (digital input 0 low)
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds frame; same measurement layout with the digital-input byte 0x00 (PIR idle). Expected produced by running reference/upstream-codec.js (entree.value[0][0]=0).

uplink.description: measurement frame: motion detected with cumulative event count
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds frame; measurement frame with digital-input block 0x01 (bit0=1, motion) followed by counter block 0x04 (channel 0) holding a 32-bit big-endian count 0x0000012C=300. Expected produced by running reference/upstream-codec.js (entree.value[0][0]=1, compte0.value[0]=300).

Synthetic in-bounds frame; measurement frame carrying only the counter block 0x04 (channel 0), 32-bit big-endian count 0x00000007=7. Expected produced by running reference/upstream-codec.js (compte0.value[0]=7).

Synthetic in-bounds frame; byte0=0x81 selects a non-timestamped life frame, then tensionv=0x0C80=3200 mV (-> 3.2 V) and tensionc=0x0C00. Expected produced by running reference/upstream-codec.js (tensionv.value=3.2).

uplink.description: device error frame yields an error
uplink._note: Synthetic frame; byte0=0x8E selects the ATIM error frame (low nibble 0xE). Upstream reference/upstream-codec.js classifies this as 'Trame d'erreur'; we surface it as a decode error since it carries no measurement.
